query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "a stylometric application of large language models" computer science
rationale: The candidate paper focuses on model compression techniques (quantization and binarization) to reduce LLM size, whereas the user's question concerns "stylometric application," which involves using LLMs to analyze writing style and authorship. These are distinct constructs within computer science with no overlap in mechanism, variables, or empirical population, failing to meet any inclusion criteria (a-f) and falling under the "Distinct construct" rejection rule.
